title: Low-Cost Convergence and Restoration Cost Explosion
one_sentence: This module explains how repeated low-cost operation generates path fixation and later restoration cost explosion.
core_thesis: A path becomes practically irreversible not only when return is costly, but because repeated low-cost operation removes exception pressure, strengthens fixation, and makes restoration increasingly expensive.

core_compression.operational_cost: C_op(P) = cost to continue along selected path
core_compression.restoration_cost: C_rest(P) = cost to abandon, revise, or reopen fixed path
core_compression.exception_compression: E(P)↓ reduces special handling and lowers operational cost
core_compression.low_cost_convergence: path repeatedly selected because continuation is cheaper
core_compression.path_fixation: repeated selection structurally privileges path P
core_compression.core_chain: E(P)↓ -> C_op(P)↓ -> repeated selection↑ -> F(P)↑ -> C_rest(P)↑

axioms.A1: Operational cost and restoration cost must be separated.

diagnostic_regimes.editable_efficiency: low operational cost with alternatives still exercised
diagnostic_regimes.low_cost_convergence: exception frequency and operational cost fall while repeated selection rises
diagnostic_regimes.silent_fixation: routine succeeds, exceptions are compressed, alternatives neglected
diagnostic_regimes.restoration_cost_explosion: fixation raises restoration cost beyond practical return threshold
diagnostic_regimes.authority_hardening: low friction is mistaken for truth or universal adequacy
diagnostic_regimes.editability_collapse: alternatives formally describable but practically unaffordable

negative_constraints: Not claim that efficiency is bad.
Not claim that all routines are dangerous.
Not claim that low cost is always fixation.
Not business efficiency theory by itself.
Do not confuse operational cheapness with reversibility.
